guestDemoAdventurous1Desc: Steep trail, wide views, early start.
guestDemoAdventurous1MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Ridge trail loop just outside the city: steep first kilometre, open viewpoints, roots and gravel underfoot. Not climbing ropes—just honest hills most fit hikers finish in 2–3 hours with snacks.

---
🥾 Gear & safety

Trail shoes or boots, at least 1L water, wind layer at the top. Offline map helps—signal thins on the spine.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Free access; small parking lot fills by 07:30 on sunny weekends. Sunrise starts are cooler and calmer.

---
💬 Moody says

Snap the photo, then pocket the phone for ten minutes—the view rented IMAX seats for you.
guestDemoAdventurous2Desc: Long lunch with harbor views — legs up, calories back, no rush.
guestDemoAdventurous2MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Harbor-front deck restaurant: seafood towers, whole grilled fish, bitterballen for the table, Dutch gin cocktails. Big windows on the water; heaters on the pier when it chills.

---
⚓ Why it fits after a hike

Salty, high-protein, celebratory—chairs you can sink into for two hours without guilt.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Book on blue-sky days. Seagulls are professionals—guard your fries like state secrets.

---
💬 Moody says

Order the messy thing; napkins exist for exactly this moment.
guestDemoAdventurous3Desc: Golden-hour drinks and small plates — land the day softly.
guestDemoAdventurous3MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Waterfront bar at golden hour: spritz list, local beers, small plates—oysters, charcuterie, croquettes. The room slowly shifts from standing-and-chatting to lounge-and-watch-the-sky.

---
🍹 Drinks

Natural wine by the glass; negroni riffs on tap in summer.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Happy hour some weekdays 17:00–19:00. Wind picks up—jacket or borrow a blanket from the host stand.

---
💬 Moody says

Stay for one more round if the sky is still showing off.
guestDemoCultural1Desc: Guided look at the new exhibition.
guestDemoCultural1MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Museum new wing with a rotating contemporary show—think large photography, installation, or mixed media. Check the banner at the entrance for this month's focus.

---
🎧 Extras

QR audio guides; Dutch + English wall texts. Gift shop stocks exhibition posters and weirdly good postcards.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Timed tickets on busy weekends. Café upstairs is a legit reset stop between floors.

---
💬 Moody says

If one piece rents space in your head for days, the ticket already paid rent back.
guestDemoCultural2Desc: Espresso between wings — let what you saw sink in.
guestDemoCultural2MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Sculpture garden café tucked between museum wings: espresso, filter coffee, cardamom buns, light lunch salads. Glass walls look straight onto bronze pieces and clipped hedges.

---
🌿 Seating

Terrace when it is dry; mid-century chairs inside when it drizzles—both feel intentional.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Garden access sometimes needs a museum ticket—read the sign at the gate. Sunday brunch queues; weekday afternoons are softer.

---
💬 Moody says

Stare at the foam, then at a statue—let your brain file what you just saw.
guestDemoCultural3Desc: Dim lights, small band — culture without the sprint finish.
guestDemoCultural3MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Intimate canal-side jazz room: house trio on quieter nights, guest bands weekends. By-the-glass wines from Loire, Alto Adige, and a few skin-contact bottles; classic cocktails; cheese and charcuterie boards.

---
🎷 Room & sound

About 60 seats—close enough to read the bassist's face. Two sets with a breather; service goes whisper-quiet during solos.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Reservations strongly recommended after 19:00. Smart-casual dress keeps the room feeling special.

---
💬 Moody says

Hum on the walk home—if you do, I nailed the encore pick.
guestDemoDayPlanMoodyBlurb: I put this together for you — start here 👇
guestDemoFoodie1Desc: Fresh pastries and coffee to start the day.
guestDemoFoodie1MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Artisan bakery + coffee bar: sourdough loaves, butter croissants laminated in-house, seasonal fruit tarts. Espresso rotates single-origin weekly; batch brew if you are sprinting.

---
🥐 What to order

Ask for whatever left the oven in the last hour. Savory danishes vanish first on Saturdays.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Opens early; expect a friendly line 09:00–10:30 weekends. Card-only at the counter.

---
💬 Moody says

Crumbs on your sleeve count as a five-star review—lean in.
guestDemoFoodie2MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Covered market hall: 30+ stalls—Dutch cheeses, roti, oysters, Korean bowls, and herring if you are feeling brave. Built for tasting: small plates so you can mix countries in one lunch.

---
🍽️ How it works

Order from vendors, meet at the long communal tables in the middle. Most stalls are cashless; allergen cards are posted.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Rush hour 12:00–13:30—scout a table first, then divide and conquer.

---
💬 Moody says

Grab one thing you cannot pronounce—I will be smug when you love it.
guestDemoFoodie3MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Evening chef counter / pop-up dinner: open kitchen, menu that shifts every few weeks, small plates or a set menu. Wine list leans acid-forward whites, light gamay, orange bottles, natural picks.

---
🔥 Kitchen style

Live fire + local produce call-outs on the board. Vegetarian route exists with a heads-up when you book.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Reservation strongly recommended. Casual-nice dress. Price tier is splurge-okay.

---
💬 Moody says

Say yes to the server is essential bite—that is where the plot twist lives.
guestDemoMoodyAdventurous0: Earn the view — then earn lunch. 🥾
guestDemoMoodyAdventurous1: You crushed the climb — now let the harbor do the work. 🛥️
guestDemoMoodyAdventurous2: Let the day’s noise fade into golden hour. 🌇
guestDemoMoodyCultural0: Give yourself time to read every plaque 🏛️
guestDemoMoodyFoodie0: Still warm, good coffee — exactly how your day should start ☕
guestDemoMoodyFoodie1: Get here early… these sell out fast 🥐
guestDemoMoodyRelaxed1: You moved — now melt into the chair. ☕
guestDemoMoodyRelaxed2: Golden hour hits different from this spot 🌅
guestDemoMoodyRomantic0: Low lights, shared plates — keep the phones away 💕
guestDemoMoodyRomantic1: Ask for a corner table if you can — worth it 🕯️
guestDemoMoodySocial0: Easy to slide in solo or with friends — the vibe is welcoming 🎉
guestDemoMoodySocial1: Pull up a bench — someone’s always finishing a story. 🥗
guestDemoMoodySocial2: Strike up a chat at the bar; regulars love newcomers 👋
guestDemoRelaxed1Desc: Easy loops under the trees — wake the body without racing.
guestDemoRelaxed1MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

City park with loops under mature trees—wide gravel paths, a duck pond pinch, and benches every few curves. Locals walk dogs, read on blankets; there is no entry fee.

---
🗺️ Layout & vibe

Mostly flat, stroller-friendly circuits (~2 km on the outer loop). Mornings are misty-quiet; you will hear more birds than traffic from here.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Free to enter. Washrooms near the main entrance. After rain, shoes that forgive mud are a win.

---
💬 Moody says

No pace to hit—if you match steps to breath for one lap, you already won.
guestDemoRelaxed2Desc: Sit deep, sip slow — let the morning walk settle in.
guestDemoRelaxed2MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Japanese-style matcha bar: stone-ground ceremonial grade, oat and soy milk, and seasonal lattes (think sakura in spring, yuzu in winter). Pastries stay light—mochi muffins, sesame cookies, minimal sugar crash.

---
🪑 Space & vibe

Low counter, matte ceramics, soft daylight. Baristas explain matcha grades without the lecture. Laptop corners exist, but phones-down regulars get the best foam.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Mid-range spend (snack + drink). Busy 12:00–14:00—slide in a bit earlier for a slower pour.

---
💬 Moody says

Sit until the morning you already had settles in your bones—that is the whole assignment.
guestDemoRelaxed3Desc: Golden hour, one drink, nowhere to be.
guestDemoRelaxed3MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Rooftop terrace restaurant: Mediterranean-leaning small plates (mezze, grilled fish, burrata), natural wines by the glass, and bottles that lean Italian + Portuguese.

---
🌅 Why sunset works here

West-facing glass rail—the sky does the lighting design. Book ahead; walk-ins sometimes grab bar spots when lucky.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Upper-mid price tier. Even summer evenings get breezy off the water—bring a light layer.

---
💬 Moody says

One drink, one horizon, zero inbox—pretend that is the only notification that matters.
guestDemoRomantic1MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Courtyard café behind a brick arch: viennoiserie, Dutch apple tart, savory galettes at lunch. Coffee from a small Rotterdam roaster; tea list leans floral and cozy.

---
🪴 Atmosphere

Fountain murmur, ivy walls, a handful of two-tops that feel tucked away. Shared blankets appear on chilly evenings.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Weekend brunch books fast—walk-in luck often after 14:00. Card and contactless.

---
💬 Moody says

Order one pastry to split and fight over the last crumb—I am taking notes.
guestDemoRomantic2Desc: Piles of reads and vinyl in the back.
guestDemoRomantic2MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Independent bookshop—not a chain. Front tables: new fiction and translated lit. Middle aisles: essays, design, travel. Back room: curated used stacks plus a vinyl corner (jazz, soul, small Dutch indie labels).

---
🎧 Listen & browse

Preview headphones at the counter. Staff shelf tags are spicy opinions, not corporate blurbs. Friday readings sometimes—chalkboard by the stairs has the schedule.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Phones on quiet, please. Bags at the door. Monthly discount cart lives by the stairs—treasure hunt energy.

---
💬 Moody says

Hunt the title that feels like it waited for you—I will take full credit if you find it.
guestDemoRomantic3Desc: Small pours, soft music, no rush.
guestDemoRomantic3MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Low-lit wine room with 80+ bottles on display—Italy (Barbera, Etna, Chianti riserva), France (Beaujolais, Loire Chenin, modest Bordeaux), Spain (Rioja, Priorat), plus orange wines and pet-nat from small Dutch importers. Coravin pours on pricier labels.

---
🍷 How drinking works

Rotating by-the-glass list (roughly 6 whites, 6 reds, 2 skin-contact). Bottles pair with small plates: olives, anchovies, burrata, charcuterie. Corkage waived with a food order on many nights—ask when you sit.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Sommelier does table rounds—say surprise us and mean it. Reservations after 19:30; smart-casual keeps the glow right.

---
💬 Moody says

Pick one bottle you cannot pronounce—we will toast to courage and pretend we are sommeliers.
guestDemoSocial1MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Organised park run / group jog at a fixed meeting pin—about 5 km, all paces welcome, volunteer hosts. Free to join; barcode timing if you like stats.

---
👟 Who shows up

First-timers, stroller parents, and speedy folks who lap politely. Zero podium pressure unless you want it.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Quick briefing a few minutes before go-time. Bag drop is honour-system near the flag—travel light if you can.

---
💬 Moody says

High-five a stranger or just nod—both count as social XP today.
guestDemoSocial2Desc: Shared plates and easy chatter — recover together after the run.
guestDemoSocial2MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Market-hall communal tables: stalls all around you, shared benches, loud happy chaos. Order small plates from different vendors and trade bites like a very civilised buffet raid.

---
🍻 The social cheat code

Long tables = easy icebreakers—ask what someone ordered and steal a recommendation.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Peak 13:00–14:00. Wipe crumbs when you leave—staff quietly love you for it.

---
💬 Moody says

Steal a fry, share a story, blame me later.
guestDemoSocial3Desc: Loud speakers, cold drinks, big vibes.
guestDemoSocial3MoodyAbout:
📚 What kind of place is this?

Live-music bar: indie bands midweek, louder DJ nights on weekends. Craft beer taps + classic cocktails. Standing room near the stage; booths if you arrive with a plan.

---
🎶 Sound & scene

Set times often live on socials. Earplugs at the bar if you like your hearing long-term.

---
⏱️ Good to know

Cover charge some Fridays/Saturdays. 18+ after 22:00. Coat check by the door.

---
💬 Moody says

Scratchy voice tomorrow means you did nightlife correctly.
